questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A <math><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> circuit behaves like a clamped harmonic oscillator. Comparing it with a physical spring-mass damped oscillator having damping constant <math><mi>'</mi><msup><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>'</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> the correct equivalence would be:
questions.solution:
In damped oscillation ma+bv+kx=0

md2xdt2+bdxdt+kx=0…..i
In the circuit
-iR-Ldidt-qc=0
Ld2qdt2+Rdqdt+1c.q=0..ii
Comparing equation (i) and (ii)
m=L,b=R,k=1c

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A series LCR circuit driven by 300 V at a frequency of 50 Hz contains a resistance R=3 kΩ, an inductor of inductive reactance XL=250π Ω and an unknown capacitor. The value of capacitance to maximise the average power should be:
take π2=10
questions.solution:
Angular frequency, ω=2πf=2π×50=100π rad s-1

At resonance,

XC=XL⇒1ωC=ωL⇒1ωC=250π⇒C=1100π×250π F=4 μF

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II.

  List-I   List-II
(A) AC generator (I) Detects the presence of current in the circuit
(B) Galvanometer (II) Converts mechanical energy into electrical energy
(C) Transformer (III) Works on the principle of resonance in AC circuit
(D) Metal detector (IV) Changes an alternating voltage for smaller or greater value
questions.solution:
AC generator-- Converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. A→II.

Galvanometer-- Detects the presence of current in the circuit B-I.

Transformer-- Changes an alternating voltage for smaller or greater value C-IV.

Metal detector-- Works on the principle of resonance in AC circuit D-III.

questions.q: In a circuit consisting of a capacitance and a generator with alternating emf, Eg=Egosinωt, VC and IC are the voltage and current. Correct phasor diagram for such circuit is
questions.solution: In capacitor, current lead voltage by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II.

  List - I   List - II
(a) ωL>1ωC (i) Current is in phase with emf
(b) ωL=1ωC (ii) Current lags behind the applied emf
(c) ωL<1ωC (iii) Maximum current occurs
(d) Resonant frequency (iv) Current leads the emf

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
questions.solution:
(a) For xL>xC, voltage leads the current

(ii)

(b) For xL=xC, voltage & current are in same phase

(i)

(c) For xL<xC, current leads the voltage

(iv)

(d) For resonant frequency xL=xC, current is maximum

(iii)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A series LCR circuit has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> and it is connected to ac voltage of amplitude <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msub><mi>V</mi><mi>m</mi></msub></mfenced><mo> </mo><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. At frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>%</mo></math> lower than resonant frequency, the amplitude of current will be approximately
questions.solution:
For an LCR circuit. the resonant angular frequency is given by, ω0=1LC=104 rad s-1

The given frequency is 60% lower than resonant frequency. Therefore,

ω'=0.4×104=4000 rad s-1

Reactance of the capacitor at given frequency,

XC=ω'C-1=250 Ω.

Reactance of the inductor at given frequency,

XL=ω'L=40 Ω

Now the amplitude of the current in the given circuit will be,

i0=V0R2+XC'-XL'2=50102+250-402=238 mA

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
For a series LCR circuit, I vs ω curve is shown 

(a) To the left of ωr, the circuit is mainly capacitive.
(b) To the left of ωr, the circuit is mainly inductive.
(c) At ωr, impedance of the circuit is equal to the resistance of the circuit.
(d) At ωr, impedance of the circuit is 0.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
questions.solution:
The impedance of an AC circuit is given by, Z=R2+XC-XL2.

For current to be maximum Z should be minimum. The value of Z will be minimum when XC-XL=0. Therefore, the value of Z=R. Hence, statement C is correct and statement D is wrong.

To the left of ωr i.e. when ω<ωr, XC>XL. Therefore, the circuit is mainly capacitive. Hence, statement A is correct and statement B is wrong.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a series <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mi>R</mi></math> circuit with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>L</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mi>R</mi></math>, power factor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. If a capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>C</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>L</mi></msub></math> is added to the circuit the power factor becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> will be :
questions.solution:
In case of LR circuit:

Given: XL=R

P1=cosϕ=RZ=RR2+XL2=12

In case of LCR circuit:

P2=cosϕ=RZ=RR2+XL-XC2=1

Therefore,

P1P2=1:2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A capacitor is connected to a 20 V battery through a resistance of 10Ω. It is found that the potential difference across the capacitor rises to 2 V in 1 μs. The capacitance of the capacitor is ________μF.
Given In 109=0.105
questions.solution:
V=V01-e-t/RC

2=201-e-1μs10C

110=1-e-1×10-610C

e-1×10-610C=910

1×10-610C=ln109

C=1×10-610×ln109=10-71.05=11.05μF=100105μF=0.95μF

∴VAB=i×4=4 V ∴VAC=VCB=2 Volt

∴q on 4μF=CVAC=8μC

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A condenser of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> capacitance is charged steadily from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. Which of the following graph represents correctly the variation of potential difference<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>V</mi></mfenced></math> across its plates with respect to the charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>Q</mi></mfenced></math> on the condenser?
questions.solution:
The charge on a condenser is given by Q=CV.

⇒V=1CQ

Since the capacitance depends on the construction of the condenser, it is constant. Therefore, the graph will be straight line with slope =1C.
Slope =1C=12×10-6=5×105

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two metallic plates form a parallel plate capacitor. The distance between the plate is 'd'. A metal sheet of thickness d2 and of area equal to area of each plate is introduced between the plates. What will be the ratio of the new capacitance to the original capacitance of the capacitor?
questions.solution:
Before the metal sheet is inserted, capacitance is given by C=ε0Ad.

After the sheet is inserted, the system is equivalent to two capacitors in series, each of capacitance C'=ε0Ad4=4C.
The equivalent capacity is now 1C'=14C+14C⇒C'=2C.

The ratio is CC'=12=1:2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A force of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> acts on a charged particle placed between two plates of a charged capacitor. If one plate of capacitor is removed, then the force acting on that particle will be.
questions.solution:
Electric field due to each plate, E1=E2=σ2ε0=Q2Aε0

Net electric field between the plates, Enet=E1+E2=QAε0

Force on charged particle between the plates, F1=qEnet=qQAε0=10 N

Now, in second case, the net electric field, E=σ2ε0 =Q2Aε0=5 N

Force on charged particle, F2=qE=qQ2Aε0=5 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two capacitors, each having capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> are connected in series. The space between one of the capacitors is filled with dielectric material of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> such that the equivalence capacitance of the system became <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> will be :
questions.solution:
When a dielectric is inserted between the sheets of a capacitor, its capacitance becomes KC.

Now the equivalent capacitance in series combination will be,

Ceq=C×KCC1+K⇒24=40K1+K⇒K=1.5

questions.q: In the experiment of Ohm's law, a potential difference of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> is applied across the end of a conductor of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and diameter of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>00</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The measured current in the conductor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>00</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The maximum permissible percentage error in the resistivity of the conductor is :-
questions.solution:
R=ρℓA=VI
ρ=AVIℓ=πd2 V4Iℓ  A=πd24
∴  Δρρ=2Δdd+ΔVV+ΔII+Δℓℓ
Δρρ=20.015.00+0.15.0+0.012.00+0.110.0
Δρρ=0.004+0.02+0.005+0.01
Δρρ=0.039
% error =Δρρ×100=0.039×100=3.90%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : A uniform wire of resistance 80 Ω is cut into four equal parts. These parts are now connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance of the combination will be 5 Ω.

Statement II : Two resistance 2R and 3R are connected in parallel in an electric circuit. The value of thermal energy developed in 3R and 2R will be in the ratio 3:2.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both statement I and statement II are correct
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
questions.solution:
Statement 1- R=80 Ω

When it is cut into 4 equal parts, the resistance of each part will be,

R1=R2=R3=R4=20 Ω

When these resistances are connected in parallel,

Req =204=5 Ω

Hence, the statement I is correct.

Statement 2 -

When the resistances are connected in parallel, the potential difference between them is same. Therefore, the power consumed can be written as,

P=V2R

⇒P1P2=R2R1=23

Hence, statement II is incorrect.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The combination of two identical cells, whether connected in series or parallel combination provides the same current through an external resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>. The value of internal resistance of each cell is
questions.solution:
When the cells are connected in series is=2ε2r+R=2ε2r+2

When they are connected in parallel, the emf is εp=εr+εr1r+1r=ε and the effective internal resistance will be rp=r×rr+r=r2. Therefore, the current will be,

ip=εR+r2=ε2+r2=2ε4+r

Since, is=ip⇒2ε2r+2=2ε4+r⇒r=2 Ω

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A student is provided with a variable voltage source V, a test resistor RT=10Ω, two identical galvanometers G1 and G2 and two additional resistors, R1=10MΩ and R2=0.001Ω. For conducting an experiment to verify ohm's law, the most suitable circuit is:
questions.solution:
A galvanometer is a tool used to either detect or quantify an electric current. The movement of a magnetic needle or a coil in a magnetic field, which is an essential component of a galvanometer, typically indicates the current and its strength.

Depending up on the resistance and the connectivity in the circuit, galvanometer can be used either as voltmeter or as an ammeter.

Voltmeter should have very high resistance, while ammeter should be having very less resistance.

Hence, the high resistor R1 should be connected in series with the galvanometer in order to measure the potential difference across the test resistor. Also, the low resistor R2 should be connected in parallel with the galvanometer in order to measure the current across the test resistor.

Thus, this combination will work properly.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The current flowing through R2 is :
questions.solution:
As can be seen from the first circuit above, CB & AB are in series which is in parallel with CA. So, the net resistance is

R'=62 Ω=3  Ω as shown in the second diagram.

From the second diagram, DC & CA is in series which is parallel to DA. Hence the resistance is

R''=6×3(6+3) Ω=2 Ω as shown in third diagram.

From third diagram, both ED & DA are in series which is parallel to EA.

The equivalent resistance as can be seen from the above simplified circuit is Req=2 Ω.

 The current I through battery, I= 82=4 A.

From the second diagram, the part EDACD has a resistance of 4 Ω, the total current will divide equally between EDACD & EA. So consider I1=2 A flowing through ED. Note that I1 will be the same current flowing in the first diagram through ED.

From first diagram, when the resistances are in parallel the current is divided in the inverse ratio. Thus,
I' through CD=39×2=23 A
I'' through R2=1223=13 A

questions.q: A square loop of side 20 cm and resistance 1 Ω is moved towards right with a constant speed v0. The right arm of the loop is in a uniform magnetic field of 5 T. The field is perpendicular to the plane of the loop and is going into it. The loop is connected to a network of resistors each of value 4Ω. What should be the value of v0 so that a steady current of 2 mA flows in the loop ?
questions.solution:
The EMF is induced in the coil when it is moving in a uniform magnetic field,
e=Bvl and the current, i=eR⇒BlvR=0.002 A
 ⇒v=0.002×RBl=0.002×4+15×0.2=0.01 m s-1=1 cm s-1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A small square loop of wire of side l is placed inside a large square loop of wire LL≫l. Both loops are coplanar and their centres coincide at point O as shown in figure. The mutual inductance of the system is
questions.solution:
Let a current, I pass through the square loop of side, L. The magnetic field at the centre O,

B=4×Magnetic field due to each side

⇒B=4×μ04π⋅IL/2sin 45∘+sin 45∘

⇒B=2μ0IπL12+12=22μ0IπL

Magnetic flux linked with the small square loop,

ϕ=BA=Bl2=22μ0Il2πL

Mutual inductance of the coil, 

M=ϕI=22μ0l2πL.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a series <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mi>R</mi></math> circuit <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>L</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mi>R</mi></math> and power factor of the circuit is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. When capacitor with capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>L</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>X</mi><mi>C</mi></msub></math> is put in series, the power factor becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
In case of L-R circuit

Z=XL2+R2 Therefore, power factor

P1=cosϕ=RZ

As XL=R ⇒Z=2R

⇒P1=R2R⇒P1=12

In case of  L-C-R circuit

Z=R2+XL-XC2

According to the question XL=XC ⇒Z=R  ⇒P2=cosϕ=RR=1

⇒P1P2=12

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two coils of self inductance L1 and L2 are connected in series combination having mutual inductance of the coils as M. The equivalent self inductance of the combination will be
questions.solution:
Here self inductances are in series but their mutual inductances are linked oppositely.

As the inductors connected are in series opposition, hence magnetic flux will be opposing each other. Therefore,

Leq=L1+L2-2M

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A metallic conductor of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> rotates in a vertical plane parallel to east-west direction about one of its end with angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the horizontal component of earth's magnetic field is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>, then emf induced between the two ends of the conductor is
questions.solution:
Given here,  BH=0.2×10-4 T, ω=5 rad s-1 and l= 1 m.

Emf induced in a rotating conducting rod is given by,

ε=12Bωl2

Substituting the given values, we obtain

ε=12×0.2×10-4×5×12=0.5×10-4 V=50 μV

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Find the mutual inductance in the arrangement, when a small circular loop of wire of radius R is placed inside a large square loop of wire of side L(L≫R). The loops are coplanar and their centres coincide :
questions.solution:
The magnetic field due to the rectangular loop at its centre is:

B=4×μ0i4πL212+12=22μ0iπL

Therefore, the flux through the circular loop ϕ=πR2×B=22μ0R2iL

If M is the mutual inductance, then

Mdidt=dϕdt=22μ0R2Ldidt

⇒M=22μ0R2L

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The induced emf can be produced in a coil by

A. moving the coil with uniform speed inside uniform magnetic field

B. moving the coil with non uniform speed inside uniform magnetic field

C. rotating the coil inside the uniform magnetic field

D. changing the area of the coil inside the uniform magnetic field

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Induced emf is given by Faraday's law

ε=dϕdt

where ϕ is the magnetic flux.

The rate of change of flux through a circuit is defined as emf. The formula for flux is ϕ=B→·A→. In the statements A&B given in the question, the magnetic flux in the coil in uniform magnetic field does not change. So, no emf is induced.

But in statement C, the angle between the area vector and the magnetic field continuously changes hence, an emf is generated. In statement D, the area is changing in unit time so magnetic flux will change and an emf is generated.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Consider the force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> on a charge '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi></math>' due to a uniformly charged spherical shell of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> carrying charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math> distributed uniformly over it. Which one of the following statements is true for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>, if  '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi></math>' is placed at distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math> from the centre of the shell?
questions.solution:
E=O, r<R

=qQr2, r≥R

Inside F=0 and outside sphare

F=2Qq4π∈0r2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In finding the electric field using Gauss law the formula <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>E</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>q</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mo>ε</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math> is applicable. In the formula <math><msub><mrow><mo>ε</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math> is permittivity of free space, <math><mi>A</mi></math> is the area of Gaussian surface and <math><msub><mrow><mi>q</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></msub></math> is charge enclosed by the Gaussian surface. This equation can be used in which of the following situation?
options: Only when the Gaussian surface is an equipotential surface

Only when the Gaussian surface is an equipotential surface and <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>E</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced></math> is constant on the surface.
Only when <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>E</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math> constant on the surface.
For any choice of Gaussian surface.
questions.solution:
According to Gauss law of electrostatics, the electric flux through an enclosed surface is given by ϕE=qencε0

From the formula of electric flux, ϕ=E→·ds→

Also, the relation between electric field and electric potential is given by V=E→·dr→

So, the formula E→=qencε0A is only applicable when the Gaussian surface is an equipotential surface and E→ is constant on the surface.

questions.q: A cube of side a has point charges +Q located at each of its vertices except at the origin where the charge is -Q. The electric field at the centre of cube is:
questions.solution:
We can replace -Q charge at origin by +Q and -2Q.

Now due to +Q charge at every corner of cube. Electric field at center of cube is zero so now net electric field at center is only due to -2Q charge at origin.

E→=kqr→r3=1-2Qa2x^+y^+z^4πε0a233

E→=-2Qx^+y^+z^33πa2ε0

questions.q: A long cylindrical volume contains a uniformly distributed charge of density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ρ</mi></math>. The radius of cylindrical volume is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>. A charge particle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>q</mi></mfenced></math> revolves around the cylinder in a circular path. The kinetic energy of the particle is :
questions.solution:
Electric field due to a long cylinder is,

E=λ2πε0r=ρA2πε0r

If a charge q is rotating in a circle of radius r

mv2r=qE  ⇒mv2r=qρA2πε0r⇒12mv2=qρπR24πε0=qρR24ε0

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Sixty four conducting drops each of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>02</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> and each carrying a charge of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> are combined to form a bigger drop. The ratio of surface density of bigger drop to the smaller drop will be
questions.solution:
Total volume will be constant. Therefore, n4π3r3=4π3R3⇒6413r=R⇒R=4r

Final surface charge density σ'=nσ04πr24πR2=64×σ0r216r2⇒σ'σ0=41

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two identical positive charges <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> each are fixed at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></math> apart from each other. Another point charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> with mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is placed at midpoint between two fixed charges. For a small displacement along the line joining the fixed charges, the charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> executes SHM. The time period of oscillation of charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> will be
questions.solution:
Let the charged particle be displaced slightly towards right by distance xx≪d.

Q at A, q:F1=14πε0Qqd+x2, towards right

Q at B, q: F2=14πε0Qqd-x2, towards left

 F→net=F→1+F→2, will bring it towards O.F→net=14πϵ01d+x2-1d-x2i^

=-Qq4πϵ04xdd2-x22i^

Since x≪d, x2 is negligible.

F→net=-Qqπϵ0d3×xi^

Clearly, the direction of the force is opposite to the displacement & F∝x, hence it is an SHM.

Acceleration of charged particle is given as,

a=Fm=-Qqπϵ0md3x    ....1

And we know that for SHM,

a=-ω2x    ….2

Comparing 1 and 2, we get

ω=Qqπϵ0md3

Hence, the time period, T=2πω=2ππϵ0md3Qq

Therefore, T=4π3ε0ma3q0Q

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: If a charge q is placed at the centre of a closed hemispherical non-conducting surface, the total flux passing through the flat surface would be
questions.solution:
Assume a complete sphere, then flux due to a charge placed at the center of sphere is ϕ=qε0

Flux through hemisphere surface is equal to flux passing through flat surface ϕ=12×qε0=q2ε0

questions.q:
A spherically symmetric charge distribution is considered with charge density varying as

ρr=ρ034−rR for r≤R Zero  for r>R

 

Where, rr<R is the distance from the centre O (as shown in figure). The electric field at point P will be :
questions.solution:
According to Gauss's law, the electric flux through a closed surface area is equal to the charge inside the surface divided by ε0.

⇒∮E·→ds→=Qinε0

⇒E4πr2=∫0rρ034-rR4πr2drε0

⇒E4πr2=ρo4πεo34r33-r44R

⇒E=ρor4εo1-rR

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two identical metallic spheres <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> when placed at certain distance in air repel each other with a force of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>F</mi></math>. Another identical uncharged sphere <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is first placed in contact with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and then in contact with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and finally placed at midpoint between spheres <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>. The force experienced by sphere <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> will be :
questions.solution:
Let charge on A and B be qA=qB=q.

Then force acting between them is F=Kq2r2.

When C is placed in contact with A, charge on A and C will be =q2

Now C is placed in contact with B, charge on B  and C will be =q+q22=3q4

Now, force experienced by sphere C is 

F'=F2-F1=K9q216×r24-K3q28×r24=K3q4-Kq2r24×3q4

=3Kq24r2=3 F4

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If the electric potential at any point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> in space is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> volt. The electric field at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> will be :
questions.solution:
Electric potential V=3x2

Electric potential and electric field are related as E=-dVdx=-6x.

At the point 1, 0, 3 m, 

⇒E=-6×1=-6 V m-1

Thus, electric field is 6 V m-1 along negative x‐ axis.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A dipole comprises of two charged particles of identical magnitude q and opposite in nature. The mass m of the positive charged particle is half of the mass of the negative charged particle. The two charges are separated by a distance l . If the dipole is placed in a uniform electric field E→; in such a way that dipole axis makes a very small angle with the electric field, E→. The angular frequency of the oscillations of the dipole when released is given by:
questions.solution:
The data given is 

l=distance between the charges

q=charge

m=mass of the positive charge

2m=mass of negative charge 

The moment of inertia of the system is 

I=m×2mm+2ml2=2ml23

The angular frequency can be written as 

ω=pEI   ...(i)

Substituting the value of moment of inertia in the equation (i) we get 

ω=pE2ml23=3pE2ml2

The value of the dipole moment is

p=ql

Hence, the angular frequency becomes

ω=3qE2ml

This question was given bonus by NTA as none of the options matched in the original paper.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A beam of protons with speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>5</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> enters a uniform magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> to the magnetic field, the pitch of the resulting helical path of protons is close to : (Mass of the proton<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>67</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>27</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math>, charge of the proton<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>69</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>19</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
Pitch

=(Vcosθ)T

=(Vcosθ)2πmeB

=4×105cos60°2π0.3×101.67×10-271.69×1019

4 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The figure shows a region of length 'l' with a uniform magnetic field of  0.3 T in it and a proton entering the region with velocity 4×105 m s-1 making an angle 60° with the field. If the proton completes 10 revolution by the time it cross the region shown, 'l' is close to (mass of proton = 1.67×10-27 kg, charge of the proton= 1.6×10-19 C)
questions.solution:
ℓ=10×pitch

=10×vcos60°×2πmgB

ℓ=10πmvqB
Put in the value of given data we find ℓ=0.44 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The fractional change in the magnetic field intensity at a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> from centre on the axis of current carrying coil of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> to the magnetic field intensity at the centre of the same coil is: (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mo><</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
As we know,

Baxis=μ0iR22R2+x23/2  where, R=a  

Bcentre =μ0i2a

∴Baxis =μ0ia22a2+r23/2

∴ fractional change in magnetic field =μ0i2a-μ0ia22a2+r23/2μ0i2a=1-11+r2a23/2

≈1−1−32r2a2=32r2a2

Find the magnetic field at centre, when a current <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> passes through coil :
questions.solution: Recall the formula of the magnitude of the magnetic field in terms of current and permeability in free space, B=∫abμ0dNi2x=∫abμ0Nb-adxi2x=μ0Ni2(b-a)lnba.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two concentric circular loops of radii r1=30 cm and r2=50 cm are placed in X-Y plane as shown in the figure. A current I=7 A is flowing through them in the direction as shown in figure. The net magnetic moment of this system of two circular loops is approximately
questions.solution:
Net magnetic moment of the system is 

M→=-IA1k^+IA2k^       (Here currents in both circular loops are in opposite direction)

M→=-Iπ0.52k^+Iπ0.32k^   

M→=-7×22725100-9100k^=-2216100k^

 M→=-3.52k^ A m2

=-72k^ A m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A proton and an alpha particle of the same velocity enter in a uniform magnetic field which is acting perpendicular to their direction of motion. The ratio of the radii of the circular paths described by the alpha particle and proton is
questions.solution:
Radius of circular path of charged particle q of mass m moving with velocity v in magnetic field B is given by r=mvqB

rαrp=4mv2eBmveB=2:1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Two long parallel conductors S1 and S2 are separated by a distance 10 cm and carrying currents of 4 A and 2 A respectively. The conductors are placed along x-axis in X-Y plane. There is a point P located between the conductors (as shown in figure).
A charge particle of 3π coulomb is passing through the point P with velocity v→=2i^+3j^ m s-1; where i^ & j^ represents unit vector along x & yaxis respectively.
The force acting on the charge particle is 4π×10-5-xi^+2j^ N. The value of x is
questions.solution:
Net magnetic field at P is BNet=μ0×i12πr1−μ0i22πr2-k^

BNet =μ042π×4100-μ022π×6100

=μ0π×10012-16

=μ0π×10042×6

=μ0×1003π-k^

So, force acting on charge particle isF→=qv→×B→=3π2i^+3j^μ0×1003π-k^

=4π×10-72i^+3j^×100-k^

=4π×10-52j^-3i^

∴  x=3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
An infinitely long hollow conducting cylinder with radius R carries a uniform current along its surface.

Choose the correct representation of magnetic field Bas a function of radial distance r from the axis of cylinder.
questions.solution:
According to Ampere's law,

∮B→·dl→=μ0i

Inside the hollow cylinder, current will be zero. Therefore, magnetic field will also be zero.

Outside the cylinder,

B2πr=μ0i⇒B=μ0i2πr⇒B∝1r

Hence, the correct answer is D.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For a moving coil galvanometer, the deflection in the coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>05</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi></math> when a current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mA</mi></math> is passed through it. If the torsional constant of suspension wire is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>rad</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the magnetic field is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and the number of turns in the coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn></math>, the area of each turn (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>cm</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> ) is :
questions.solution:
Given: θ=0.05 rad, I=10 mA, K=4×10-5 N m rad-1, B=0.01 T, N=200

The torsional constant of a wire used in a moving coil galvanometer is given by 

K=NIABθ, where, A is area of each turn

So, A=KθNIB=4×10-5×0.05200×10×10-3×0.01

Thus, A=10-4 m2=1 cm2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A long straight wire of circular cross-section (radius a) is carrying steady current I. The current I is uniformly distributed across this cross-section. The magnetic field is
options: inversely proportional to r in the region r<a and uniform throughout in the region  r>a
directly proportional to r in the region r<a and inversely proportional to r in the region r>a

Zero in the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi><mo><</mo><mi>a</mi></math> and inversely proportional to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> in the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi><mo>></mo><mi>a</mi></math>
uniform in the region r<a and inversely proportional to distance r from the axis, in the region r>a
questions.solution:
The formula to calculate the magnetic field inside the conducting wire is given by

Bin =μ0Jr2   ...(1)

The formula to calculate the magnetic field outside the conducting wire is given by

Bout =μ0Ja22r   ...(2)

From equation (1), it can be concluded that the magnetic field is directly proportional to the radial distance inside the wire, whereas from equation (2), it can be concluded that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the radial distance outside the wire.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A perfectly diamagnetic sphere has a small spherical cavity at its centre, which is filled with a paramagnetic substance. The whole system is placed in a uniform magnetic field B→. Then the field inside the paramagnetic substance is:
options: much large than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> and parallel to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>

much large than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> but opposite to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>
questions.solution: When magnetic field is applied diamagnetic substance produces magnetic field in opposite direction so net magnetic field will be zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct?
(A) Electric monopoles do not exist whereas magnetic monopoles exist.
(B) Magnetic field lines due to a solenoid at its ends and outside cannot be completely straight and confined.
(C) Magnetic field lines are completely confined within a toroid.
(D) Magnetic field lines inside a bar magnet are not parallel.
(E) χ=-1 is the condition for a perfect diamagnetic material, where χ is its magnetic susceptibility.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
Statement (C) is correct because, the magnetic field outside the toroid is zero and they form closed loops inside the toroid itself.

Statement (E) is correct because we know that super conductors are materials inside which the net magnetic field is always zero and they are perfect diamagnetic.

μr=1+χ

χ=-1

μr=0

For superconductors.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
An electron with energy 0.1keV moves at right angle to the earth's magnetic field of 1×10-4Wbm-2. The frequency of revolution of the electron will be
(Take mass of electron =9.0×10-31 kg)
questions.solution:
The frequency of revolution of the electron is f=eB2πm, here, e is charge of electron, B is magnetic field and m is mass of electron.

So, f=1.6×10-19×10-42π×9×10-31=2.8×106 Hz

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: For diamagnetic substance -1≤x<0, where x is the magnetic susceptibility.

Statement II: Diamagnetic substance when placed in an external magnetic field, tend to move from stronger to weaker part of the field. In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options give below.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are False
Statement I is correct but Statement II is false
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
questions.solution:
There are no atomic dipoles in diamagnetic materials because the resultant magnetic moment of each atom is zero due to paired electrons. Diamagnetic materials are repelled by a magnet. These substances are weakly repelled by the field, so in a nonuniform field, these substances have a tendency to move from a strong to a weak part of the external magnetic field.

For diamagnetic substance (-1<x<0) and a diamagnetic substance is repelled by the magnetic field.
